title: Ilysanthes antipoda
taxonomy: Ilysanthes antipoda is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. This family is commonly known as the figwort family. The Scrophulariaceae family is a group of flowering plants that were previously classified in the order Scrophulariales. However, the APG IV system places them in the order Lamiales. This classification is part of the larger system of plant taxonomy and nomenclature.

intro: Ilysanthes antipoda, also known as the New Caledonian blue verbena, is a flowering plant species native to New Caledonia, characterized by its blue to purple flowers and aromatic foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ilysanthes antipoda include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
